generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components – mechanical parts of the first axis
instance MotorActingComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "motor of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot first vertical axis (axis 1)"
]
instance GearwheelActingComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "gearwheel part of the first axis"
]
instance CycloidalGearboxActingComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "cycloidal gearbox connecting motor to robot arm"
]
instance RobotAxis1ActingComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 serial robot used for machining"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance IMCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "current sensor feeding motor input current I_M"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IMCurrentDataStream
]
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "position encoder on the motor"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataStream
]
instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "secondary encoder behind the gearbox"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataStream
]
instance EndEffectorAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "angle encoder that measures the end‑effector position"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EndEffectorAngleDataStream
]
instance DerivedTorqueSignal : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "derived torque signal obtained from the machine controller"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueSignalDataStream
]
instance DerivedAngularVelocitySignal : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "derived angular velocity signal obtained from the machine controller"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocitySignalDataStream
]
instance DerivedAccelerationSignal : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "derived acceleration signal obtained from the machine controller"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AccelerationSignalDataStream
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑virtual interaction – MQTT data streams
instance IMCurrentDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IMCurrentSensor
]
instance MotorPositionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]
instance GearboxPositionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]
instance EndEffectorAngleDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EndEffectorAngleEncoder
]
instance TorqueSignalDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DerivedTorqueSignal
]
instance AngularVelocitySignalDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DerivedAngularVelocitySignal
]
instance AccelerationSignalDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DerivedAccelerationSignal
]

// C10: Models and Data – SysML structure, friction models and simulation models
instance SystemPhysicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML component‑connector diagram of the first axis (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASEnablerInstance
]
instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Coulomb‑Viscous friction model used for real‑time identification"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]
instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Stribeck friction model used for real‑time identification"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]
instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dahl friction model used for real‑time identification"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]
instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "LuGre friction model used for real‑time identification"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]
instance MotorSimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink executable model of the motor"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]
instance GearwheelSimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink executable model of the gearwheel"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]
instance GearboxSimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink executable model of the gearbox"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]
instance FullSystemSimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink executable model of the complete first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngineInstance
]

// Data instances – measured, simulated, and aggregated signals
instance MeasuredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "measured motor input current I_M"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData IMCurrentDataStream
]
instance MeasuredMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "measured motor position angle"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataStream
]
instance MeasuredGearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "measured gearbox position angle"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionDataStream
]
instance MeasuredEndEffectorAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "measured end‑effector angle"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData EndEffectorAngleDataStream
]
instance MeasuredTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "measured torque signal"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueSignalDataStream
]
instance MeasuredVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "measured angular velocity signal"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocitySignalDataStream
]
instance MeasuredAccelerationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "measured acceleration signal"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData AccelerationSignalDataStream
]
instance SimulatedMotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "simulated output of the motor model (r_M)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DashboardServerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorSimModel
]
instance SimulatedGearboxOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "simulated output of the gearbox model (r_G)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DashboardServerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxSimModel
]
instance SimulatedFullSystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "simulated output of the full system model (r_out)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DashboardServerInstance
    DTDFVocab:fromData FullSystemSimModel
]

// C11: Enablers – tooling, brokers, and data exchange mechanisms
instance SimulinkEngineInstance : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink runtime for executing system and friction models"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, TraceComparisonService, DataAggregationService
]
instance MQTTBrokerInstance : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker for live data streaming from sensors to the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService
]
instance AASEnablerInstance : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell for storing and retrieving asset metadata"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]
instance DashboardServerInstance : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "web‑based dashboard for unified visualization of friction losses and control accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]
instance ParameterExchangeEnablerInstance : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "dynamic exchange of friction model parameters during operation"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService
]

// C6: Services – real‑time identification, comparison, abstraction, and visualization
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "continuous real‑time identification of joint friction models (Coulomb‑Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre)"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionEstimatesInsight, StoreUpdatedModelParametersAction
]
instance TraceComparisonService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "comparison of measured and simulated traces"
    DTDFVocab:provides TraceComparisonInsight, AdjustControllerGainsAction
]
instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "data aggregation and abstraction for the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides AggregatedDataInsight
]
instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "visualization in a unified dashboard providing engineers insight into friction losses and control accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizeFrictionLossesInsight
]

// C17: Insights and Actions
// Insights
instance FrictionEstimatesInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "friction estimates fed back to engineers for decision making"
]
instance TraceComparisonInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "comparison of measured and simulated motor and system outputs"
]
instance AggregatedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "aggregated data representation for digital shadow"
]
instance VisualizeFrictionLossesInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "visual representation of friction losses on the dashboard"
]

// Actions
instance StoreUpdatedModelParametersAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "stores updated friction model parameters for future control loops"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance AdjustControllerGainsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "adjusts controller gains based on friction estimates"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance SelectToolParametersAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "selects tool parameters for machining based on friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance ScheduleMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "schedules maintenance tasks when friction trends indicate wear"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Real‑time, continuous acquisition and processing; data streams and simulation runs are synchronized at the sampling period of the motor current (typically sub‑millisecond to millisecond resolution), enabling near‑instantaneous updates of friction estimates."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Sensor layer (current and encoder sensors) → MQTT broker → Processing layer (digital shadow: simulation and data‑calculation models) → Visualization layer (dashboard). The Asset Administration Shell represents the physical asset and provides contextual information. All components are orchestrated to ingest live data, compute simulated traces, and present results to users."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "Design (structural SysML model creation), Construction (implementation of simulation and friction models, integration of MQTT data streams), Operation (continuous identification and visualization during robot service), Evolution (dynamic replacement of friction models and adaptation to wear)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT provides traceability between source traces, simulation models, and data traces. Validation is performed by comparing simulated outputs against measured sensor data; discrepancies are used to update friction model parameters. The use of both white‑box analytical models and black‑box data‑driven models mitigates model uncertainty and accommodates changes due to wear or temperature."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "One‑way MQTT protocol for sensor data transmission; HTTP/HTTPS for dashboard communication; data serialized in JSON; the Asset Administration Shell uses an OpenAPI interface exposing asset metadata."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with the industrial asset ecosystem via the Asset Administration Shell, allowing sharing of asset models and data with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Use of SysML (IEEE 33-2014) for MBSE, Asset Administration Shell (IEC 62832) as standard for asset representation, MQTT (ISO/IEC 20922) for data exchange, and Simulink for simulation of physical dynamics."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The DT is passive and does not issue control commands, eliminating risk of remote execution on the PT. Physical safety is enforced by the robot’s safety cell (range limits ±90°) and the Siemens SINUMERIK controller. No explicit cybersecurity measures are described; the system relies on secure network isolation for MQTT traffic."
]
